description: Workspace, section and domain model for supporting multiple work scopes without forking the intelligence pipeline.
core_rule: Every workspace keeps the core intelligence modules: source management, candidate pool, daily reports, weekly reports, historical archive, entity timeline archive, quality archive, SQL exports, users and audit. Optional modules are database-registered and disabled by default.

workspace.purpose: A user-facing work scope that still uses the same intelligence pipeline.
owns: isolated unified label policy for model tagging
feedback policy for viewer collaboration permissions

section.purpose: A database-registered core page or optional plugin page inside a workspace.

domain.purpose: The subject matter of intelligence content, such as AI, hardware, semiconductor, robotics or policy.

fields: code (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$, unique across enabled and disabled workspaces)
provisioning: All core_sections are registered and enabled for the new workspace.
config_json.label_policy starts from the default ai_sql_categories policy and is then edited per workspace via PATCH /api/workspaces/{code}/label-policy.
config_json.feedback_policy starts from the default viewer feedback policy and is then edited per workspace via PATCH /api/workspaces/{code}/feedback-policy.
Existing super admins receive owner memberships.
The creator receives an owner membership even when the creator is editor_admin rather than super_admin.
sort_order is appended after existing workspaces.
rules: Custom workspaces are database records; the startup seed only maintains the built-in planning_intel and ai_tools definitions and must never disable or overwrite custom workspaces.
New workspaces integrate sources through the shared pool: enable existing sources via workspace_source_links or create new ones via POST /api/sources.
Creating a workspace must not fork the pipeline, the SQL contract or the taxonomy files.
Disabled workspaces are hidden from GET /api/workspaces but data is retained.
planning_intel cannot be disabled.
Startup seed maintains only built-in planning_intel and ai_tools and must not overwrite custom workspaces.

membership_management.min_role: workspace admin or owner; super_admin bypasses membership
rules: GET /api/workspaces filters to enabled memberships for non-super_admin users.
Member removal disables the membership row instead of deleting history.
The last enabled owner in a workspace cannot be removed.

workspace.member.upsert and workspace.member.remove audit logs must include before/after membership snapshots so /api/identity/permission-changes can explain the diff and /api/identity/permission-rollbacks can restore the previous state.
The frontend member-management view must explain role impact and disable the remove action for the last owner.

section_groups.notes: Navigation is grouped vertically by editorial job. The group lives in workspace_sections.config_json.group and is returned by the sections API; the frontend renders groups dynamically.

scope_fields.workspace_code: Which workspace a processed object belongs to. Data source definitions are reusable and are enabled through workspace_source_links.
scope_fields.domain_code: Which intelligence domain the content belongs to.
scope_fields.visibility_scope: Who can see or sync the object.
scope_fields.sync_policy: How the object moves between public, intranet and local deployments.

rules: Do not use domain_code as a UI workspace boundary.
Do not create a separate frontend/backend for any workspace.
Do not remove source management, candidate pool, daily reports or weekly reports from any workspace.
Do not duplicate data source definitions per workspace; reuse data_sources through workspace_source_links.
New work scopes are workspaces; optional pages/tools are workspace_sections with enabled=false by default.
The frontend must load workspace list and enabled sections from backend APIs; hard-coded navigation is only a temporary scaffold.
Do not show tool_catalog, tool_runs or standalone_topics unless workspace_sections explicitly enables them.
First-level and second-level news labels are configured once per workspace in workspaces.config_json.label_policy and constrain model-generated news categories.

planning_intel defaults to ai_sql_categories from config/taxonomy/news_categories.json. ai_tools defaults to ai_tools_categories with 工具新功能/工具新案例/工具新技术 and cursor/claude code/opencode/codex as second-level labels under each primary label.
Source-side tags use planning_source_tags from config/taxonomy/source_tags.json and are only data-source metadata/hints; they must not become generated_news.category.
First-level and second-level labels are the directory for clustering and recommendation; do not add a separate tool management page for that.
Data APIs that list reusable sources may filter by workspace_code through workspace_source_links.
Workspace membership controls access to a desktop, while global roles still control privileged actions.

Daily report item reactions, ratings and comments use workspaces.config_json.feedback_policy: viewer can write only when viewer_can_react/viewer_can_rate/viewer_can_comment is true; member and above can still collaborate when viewer feedback is closed.
viewer_can_edit remains false in v1; report item editing still requires workspace member+.
notify_on_comment is active for daily report comments; notify_on_publish is active for daily_report.published and weekly_report.published notifications and is still off by default.
Changing feedback_policy writes audit_logs with action workspace.feedback_policy.update.
The /users policy view provides a visual editor for viewer_can_react/viewer_can_rate/viewer_can_comment/notify_on_comment/notify_on_publish and requires an explicit impact confirmation before PATCH.
The /users policy view must show feedback_policy before/after diffs from GET /api/identity/permission-changes and may rollback selected changes via POST /api/identity/permission-rollbacks.
The frontend must always send viewer_can_edit=false even if a stale response contains another value.

resolution_order: 2. associated domain pack (config/domain_packs/*.json, matched through workspaces.default_domain_code)
3. built-in AI default (identical to the planning_intel rules; keeps existing AI workspaces byte-for-byte unchanged)
resolved_fields: scoring mode and prior keywords (AI workspaces keep the original noise rules + global content scorer; other workspaces use neutral admission with pack/policy priors and no AI noise down-weighting)
category fallback keyword rules (built-in AI/ai_tools keyword tables are the built-in default data)
rendition board taxonomy (ai_builtin/domain_pack/label_policy/single_group; body_json records board_taxonomy_source)
generation fallback persona text (defaults to workspace.name; planning_intel is locked to the original 规划部 wording)
content_policy_resolution.domain_pack_listing_endpoint: GET /api/domain-packs lists consumable packs (boards/label_sets/category_keywords/scoring summary; associate_by=default_domain_code)
content_policy_resolution.company_sql_gating: Company SQL export is only available when the resolved policy is company-SQL capable: news_format_code=company_sql_v1 and allowed_primary_categories is a subset of the AI 10 categories. Other workspaces get preflight report-level error workspace_not_company_sql and HTTP 400 from the export APIs (batch entries fail per item); there is no silent category remapping.

domain_pack_example.rule: The startup seed reads flat JSON files in config/domain_packs and registers label_sets plus labels; domain pack config must not fork the pipeline. Packs are consumed at runtime through content_policy_resolution (scoring priors, boards with fallback_board, category_keywords) and listed by GET /api/domain-packs; consumption semantics are documented in config/domain_packs/README.md.
